sections.paragraph:
ἀντιβάλλω (s. βάλλω; Thu. et al.; SEG XXVI, 1295 [II a.d.]; pap, LXX) lit. ‘put/place against’, to be engaged in an exchange, exchange metaph. discuss τὶ πρός τινα (2 Macc 11:13 ἀ. πρὸς ἑαυτόν) τίνες οἱ λόγοι οὓς ἀντιβάλλετε πρὸς ἀλλήλους; what are the words you are exchanging with each other? i.e. what is the subject of your discussion? Lk 24:17 (Vi. Pol. [IV a.d.] 11, 3 πρὸς ἀλλήλους κατʼ ἰδίαν ἀντέβαλλον; Theophanes Conf., Chron. 461, 18 ἀντιβάλλειν πρὸς ἀλλήλους=dispute).—M-M. TW. Spicq.

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

metaph. metaph. = metaphor(ically)

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Theophanes Conf Theophanes Conf , VIII–IX a.d.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 88
